cards.back: The process of improving the quality of an image that has been degraded by noise, distortions, or other forms of corruption.
Gaussian noise, Salt and Pepper noise, Impulsive noise, and Shot noise are common types of noise that affect image quality.
The noise models used for restoration include the Gaussian model, the Poisson model, and the generalized Gaussian model.
Common restoration algorithms include the Wiener filter, the kurtosis-based filter, and the gradient domain filtering techniques.

cards.front: Image Restoration in Presence of Noise Only
cards.back: Restoration in the presence of noise only involves the use of noise models and algorithms that can effectively remove noise from an image without affecting its original content.
